violations.explanation: Incorrect POM access pattern. The page additions file defines plan_pause_info as a property method on PrintHistoryPage class, so it should be accessed as print_history_page.plan_pause_info, not print_history_page.elements.plan_pause_info. The .elements. namespace is only used when locators are stored as string attributes in the Elements inner class, but the page additions defines property methods that return Locator objects directly.
Incorrect POM access pattern. Same issue as V001 - should access property method directly without .elements. namespace.
Incorrect POM access pattern. billing_cycle_period_card is defined as a property method, not an Elements class attribute.

violations.expected_code:

violations.explanation: CRITICAL structural error. The page additions file defines a standalone Elements class, but PrintHistoryPage already has an Elements inner class. The additions should be attributes to add to the EXISTING Elements class, not a new standalone class. The merge instructions say 'Add Elements class attributes to PrintHistoryPage.Elements inner class' but the code defines a new class instead of just listing the attributes to add.

fix_details.description: Remove the 'class Elements:' declaration and docstring. Keep only the attribute assignments as they will be added to the existing PrintHistoryPage.Elements inner class.
fix_details.current_structure:
class Elements:
    """Locator selectors for PrintHistoryPage."""
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    # ... etc
fix_details.required_structure:
# Attributes to add to PrintHistoryPage.Elements inner class:
plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
# ... etc

violations.explanation: The property methods are actually CORRECT in their implementation - they properly reference self.elements.X and call self.page.locator(). However, this violation is marked because the test script incorrectly accesses these as print_history_page.elements.plan_pause_info instead of print_history_page.plan_pause_info. The property methods are correct, but the test script usage is wrong. This is a cross-file consistency issue.

fix_details.note: Property methods are correctly implemented. The issue is in the test script which incorrectly uses .elements. to access these properties. Fix the test script, not the page additions.

fix_details.replace_with:
Merge instructions:
1. Open pages/print_history_page.py in the AURA-FRAMEWORK repository
2. Locate the existing 'class Elements:' inner class (should be near the top of PrintHistoryPage class)
3. Add the following 13 attributes to the Elements class (after existing attributes):
   plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
   billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
   complimentary_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-progress-bar']"
   complimentary_pages_value = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-value']"
   complimentary_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon']"
   complimentary_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-message']"
   complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']:near([data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon'])"
   additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
   additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
   additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
   additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
   additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']:near([data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon'])"
   total_printed_pages = "[data-testid='total-printed-pages']"
4. Scroll to the end of the PrintHistoryPage class body (before the final closing)
5. Add all 13 property methods listed in this file
6. Save the file
7. Run the test script to verify all locators work
8. If any locators fail, inspect the actual HTML and update the data-testid values

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace all instances of print_history_page.elements.X with print_history_page.X for the three locators that are incorrectly accessed via .elements. namespace (plan_pause_info appears twice, billing_cycle_period_card once). The page additions define these as property methods, not Elements class attributes, so they should be accessed directly without .elements.

Restructure the page additions file to remove the standalone 'class Elements:' declaration. The file should only contain the attribute assignments (without the class wrapper) since these will be added to the existing PrintHistoryPage.Elements inner class. Keep the property methods as-is since they are correctly implemented.
Add explicit timeout=30000 parameter to the two expect().not_to_be_empty() assertions that are missing it. All expect() calls should have explicit timeouts for consistency.

Expand the merge instructions to provide detailed step-by-step guidance including exact locations in the target file, what existing structure to look for, and how to handle the merge. This makes integration easier and less error-prone.

Enhance the module docstring to provide more context about the purpose of these additions, what feature they support, and why they're needed. Include test case title and detailed description of the elements being added.
